title: Creative Mentoring & Day Program for Homeless Youth
description: A welcoming daytime drop-in space for young people moving through and beyond homelessness — creative mentoring, education, art, recreation, meals, and referrals. Serves about 65 youth a day. Open Tuesday–Friday, 9am–2pm.
engage: Visit P:ear at 338 NW 6th Ave, Portland (Tue–Fri 9am–2pm) or call 503-228-6677. Review eligibility and what to expect at pearmentor.org before your first visit.
content:
Programs

  • Core Programs: education, arts, and recreation
  • Meals: over 23,000 served a year
  • Support: referrals and parenting support

Who It's For

Young people experiencing or transitioning out of homelessness.

Hours

Tuesday–Friday, 9am–2pm, at 338 NW 6th Ave, Portland.

title: Works Job Training Program
description: P:ear's job-training and paid work-experience program helping homeless and transitional youth build employment skills and a work history.
engage: Ask P:ear staff about the Works program during a visit, or call 503-228-6677.
content:
Program

The Works programs give young people job training and real work experience — building skills, confidence, and employment history as they move beyond homelessness.

Who It's For

Homeless and transitional youth ready to build job skills.

title: Recovery Meetings & Community Center
description: A free, inclusive recovery community center in NW Portland hosting 100+ recovery meetings a week (AA, NA, and other peer groups), plus online meeting access. Open to anyone seeking recovery from substance use and mental health challenges for 60+ years.
engage: Drop in at 909 NW 24th Ave, Portland, or call 503-222-5756. Meeting schedules at alanoclub.org. All services are free.
content:
What's Offered

  • 100+ in-person recovery meetings weekly (AA, NA, and more)
  • Access to online recovery meetings
  • Specialized programs: Recovery Toolkit, Peer Connect (peer matching), Artists in Recovery, Project Red (overdose prevention)

Who It's For

Anyone seeking recovery from substance use and/or mental health disorders.

Cost

Free.

title: Free Recovery Classes & Workshops
description: A rotating lineup of free classes and workshops for people in recovery — legal aid clinics, financial literacy, arts classes, cooking workshops (Plates for the People), sound baths, and more, across multiple locations.
engage: See the current class schedule at alanoclub.org or call 503-222-5756. All classes are free.
content:
Offerings

  • Legal aid clinics
  • Financial literacy
  • Arts classes and Artists in Recovery
  • Cooking / nutrition workshops (Plates for the People)
  • Sound baths and wellness sessions

Who It's For

People in recovery seeking skills, connection, and wellness. Free and open.

title: Accessible Housing & Attendant Care
description: Affordable, barrier-free (wheelchair-accessible) apartments plus optional 24/7 resident-directed attendant care for adults with physical disabilities, across five Portland-area communities. Designed for independent living with roll-in showers, roll-under counters, and emergency systems.
engage: Apply online at quadinc.org/services/application-process, or call 503-287-4260.
content:
Services

  • Accessible Housing: studio, 1-, and 2-bedroom wheelchair-accessible apartments with open floor plans, roll-under counters, custom appliances, and roll-in showers
  • 24/7 Shared Attendant Care (optional): resident-directed help with daily living — wake-up care, cooking, medical assistance, and community activities
  • Community amenities: common rooms, gardens, and emergency assistance systems

Locations

Myers Court (N Portland), Central Station (Gresham), Rolling Green (Hillsboro), Burnside Station and Station 162 (E Portland).

Who It's For

Adults with physical disabilities seeking accessible, supportive independent living.

title: Mental Health Counseling
description: Therapy and counseling for emotional wellness from an integrative-health nonprofit, with in-person and telehealth options.
engage: Call 503-238-5203 or email info@quest-center.org to schedule mental health services.
content:
Program

Individual therapy and counseling supporting emotional and mental wellness, offered through an integrative, whole-person approach. Telehealth available.

title: FSR Addiction Services
description: Substance-use counseling and recovery support (FSR) integrated with whole-person wellness, available in person and via telehealth.
engage: Call 503-238-5203 or email info@quest-center.org for addiction services.
content:
Program

Counseling and recovery support for substance use, delivered within Quest's integrative-health model. Telehealth counseling available.

title: HIV Services
description: Specialized, integrative healthcare and wellness support for people living with HIV.
engage: Call 503-238-5203 or email info@quest-center.org to access HIV services.
content:
Program

Integrative healthcare and wellness support tailored to people living with HIV, coordinated with Quest's medical, mental health, and nutrition services.

Who It's For

People living with HIV.

title: Integrative Medical & Primary Care
description: Primary healthcare integrated with Quest's wellness services, including pain management (WISH) and nutrition support — a whole-person approach to health.
engage: Call 503-238-5203 or email info@quest-center.org to establish care.
content:
Services

  • Primary healthcare
  • WISH pain management for chronic or acute pain
  • Nutrition counseling

Care is integrated across Quest's medical, mental health, and wellness programs.

title: Transitional Housing
description: Residential transitional housing support helping Quest clients gain stability as part of their integrative health and recovery.
engage: Call 503-238-5203 or email info@quest-center.org to ask about transitional housing.
content:
Program

Transitional housing and residential support integrated with Quest's health and recovery services, helping clients stabilize.

title: Radical Hospitality Gatherings
description: Weekly hospitality gatherings offering nourishing meals, supplies, community, and genuine connection for women, trans, and nonbinary people experiencing poverty, homelessness, isolation, and street-based survival. No questions asked.
engage: Email info@rahabs-sisters.org or call 971-208-3176 to find the current gathering time and location.
content:
What's Offered

  • Nourishing hot meals
  • Clothing, hygiene, and safety supplies
  • Warm, judgment-free community and connection

Who It's For

Women, trans, and nonbinary folks facing poverty, homelessness, and isolation — including people in street-based survival economies.

How to Access

Come to a weekly gathering. Contact Rahab's Sisters for the current time and place.

title: Bilingual Latine Mental Health Services
description: Culturally-grounded, bilingual and bicultural mental health services for Latine communities — from prevention and community education to clinical therapy, offered via telehealth and mobile services. Staff are hired from the communities they serve.
engage: New patients: submit the intake form at forms.gle/BbYkErFs5Svng7mB9. Call (971) 417-6054 with questions. Existing patients use the portal at rdb.mytheranest.com.
content:
Services

  • Community Liberation Programs: prevention activities and trauma-informed community education
  • Clinical Mental Health: bilingual/bicultural therapy via telehealth and mobile services
  • Training & Consultation: culturally-responsive mental health practice for providers

Who It's For

Latine individuals, families, community leaders, and traditional health workers in Oregon.

Language

Bilingual and bicultural — services in Spanish and English.

title: Recovery Resource Directory
description: A free statewide online tool connecting people to recovery support across all 36 Oregon counties — medical care and treatment, peer services, recovery meetings, health & wellness, financial resources, and housing. Search by your situation and county, or connect with a peer supporter.
engage: Search recovery resources for free at recoverynetworkoforegon.org — pick your situation (getting sober, thriving in recovery, using more safely) and your county.
content:
What It Offers

A searchable directory built on the principle that "every path to recovery is valid," connecting people to:

  • Medical care & treatment
  • Peer services and peer supporters
  • Recovery meetings
  • Health & wellness
  • Financial resources
  • Housing & shelter

Who It's For

Anyone in recovery — those just starting, using more safely, in long-term recovery, and their family and friends. Covers all 36 Oregon counties.

title: Detox (Withdrawal Management)
description: 24/7 medically-supervised inpatient detox at the Foster Road location, providing a safe start to recovery. Accepts OHP and most major insurance.
engage: Call (503) 906-9995 for a confidential assessment and insurance verification.
content:
Program

Around-the-clock, medically-supervised inpatient withdrawal management to safely detox from alcohol and other drugs, with medication support as appropriate.

Insurance

Accepts OHP, Aetna, BCBS, Cigna, United Healthcare, and Moda. Located at Foster Road Detox, Portland.

title: Outpatient Treatment, MAT & Mental Health
description: Judgment-free outpatient addiction treatment with individual and group counseling, medication-assisted treatment (Suboxone, Vivitrol), and mental health therapy for trauma, depression, anxiety, and co-occurring disorders. Locations in Portland, Tigard, and Newberg.
engage: Call (503) 906-9995 to get started and verify coverage (OHP and most insurance accepted).
content:
Services

  • Outpatient individual and group counseling
  • Medication-assisted treatment (Suboxone, Vivitrol)
  • Mental health therapy for trauma, depression, anxiety, and co-occurring disorders
  • Peer support from certified specialists

Locations

Portland, Tigard, and Newberg.

title: DUII Diversion Program
description: State-certified DUII diversion program helping people meet court and DMV requirements, with assessment, education, and treatment.
engage: Call (503) 906-9995 to enroll in the DUII diversion program.
content:
Program

A state-certified program fulfilling court and DMV DUII requirements, including assessment, alcohol/drug education, and treatment as needed.

Who It's For

Individuals with DUII charges or diversion requirements.

title: Medical Detox (Referral-Based)
description: Focused medical detoxification in a private, campus-style setting with 24/7 clinical supervision, high staff-to-client ratio, private rooms, case management, and discharge planning. Referral-based admissions only (no walk-ins). Accepts OHP and most insurance.
engage: Admissions are referral-based by appointment — call 503-919-3199 for a referral and insurance verification. No walk-ins.
content:
Program

Medically-supervised detox with round-the-clock clinical supervision in a private, campus-style setting. Includes case management and discharge planning to connect you to next steps.

Substances Treated

Alcohol, opioids, fentanyl, heroin, cocaine, methamphetamine, prescription drugs, and hallucinogens.

Access & Insurance

Referral-based admissions only, by appointment — no walk-ins. Accepts Oregon Health Plan and most insurance. Does not provide emergency medical services.
